era: 1990-1997. Riot Grrrl-adjacent. Capitol Hill, Seattle. E=8 -- fierce, raw, grief-inflected, defiant.
associated_musicians: Mia Zapata (The Gits) -- '¡Viva Zapata!' named in her tribute

theory_nodes.concept: C/Z RECORDS ENTERS ENGINE (279): Seattle-based independent label. Also: Mudhoney, Love Battery. First non-Kill Rock Stars, non-Sub Pop Seattle label in the riot grrrl register. Seven Year Bitch on C/Z then Atlantic Records for '¡Viva Zapata!'
theory_nodes.description: C/Z Records enters at degree 279. Seattle independent infrastructure.

theory_nodes.concept: Capitol Hill feminist punk -- first Seattle (not Olympia) riot grrrl entry. Same E=8 energy as Olympia scene but different community, different institutional context, different geographic identity. Seattle vs. Olympia distinction real and important.
theory_nodes.description: Seven Year Bitch as the Capitol Hill node of the PNW feminist punk map.

theory_nodes.concept: Stefanie Sargent: founding guitarist, died June 27 1992, age 24. Recorded debut album. Loss is a defining event in band's history. Band continues, carries grief through defiance. Documented with care and factual accuracy.
theory_nodes.description: Sargent's death and the band's response: continuation as defiance.

theory_nodes.concept: '¡Viva Zapata!' (1994, Atlantic Records): title tribute to Mia Zapata of The Gits (degree 280, murdered July 7 1993). Double meaning: Emiliano Zapata and Mia Zapata. '¡Viva Zapata!' = 'Long live Zapata!' Grief through defiance. Atlantic major label; wider audience without dilution.
theory_nodes.description: The central album and its tribute name -- the bridge to degree 280.

theory_nodes.concept: 279-280 bridge: Seven Year Bitch and The Gits are connected by the deaths of Stefanie Sargent (1992) and Mia Zapata (1993). Two losses in the Capitol Hill feminist punk community within one year. '¡Viva Zapata!' names the connection explicitly.
theory_nodes.description: The explicit cross-degree bridge between 279 and 280.

cross_references.relationship: DIRECT BRIDGE. '¡Viva Zapata!' (1994) named for Mia Zapata of The Gits, murdered July 7 1993. Selene Vigil and Mia Zapata were friends. Capitol Hill feminist punk community's response to two losses (Sargent 1992, Zapata 1993) documented across two consecutive degrees.
Olympia riot grrrl vs. Capitol Hill riot grrrl-adjacent. Same E=8, different city, different label (Kill Rock Stars vs. C/Z). Both are essential to the full picture of PNW feminist punk.
E=8 fourth consecutive (276-279). Kill Rock Stars ends at 278; C/Z Records enters at 279. The E=8 streak continues across four degrees with entirely different band characters.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=5. SECOND BALAENOPTERIDAE. Combined E=13. The deepest sustained note in nature, felt more than heard, paired with Capitol Hill's fiercest feminist punk act. Two entirely different registers of intensity.

milestones: FIRST SEATTLE (NOT OLYMPIA) FEMINIST PUNK ENTRY
